#### "Recognized Leader in Data Center Intelligent PDU Solutions"

Digipower Manufacturing Inc. is recognized as a key vendor in the global data center Rack PDU market, as identified by leading international market research institutions including Technavio and Introspective Market Research.

Founded in 1999, Digipower has become a recognized leader in intelligent power distribution and remote power management solutions for critical data center environments. Based in Taiwan, our extensive PDU portfolio, including advanced per-outlet managed PDUs, supports global OEM partners and infrastructure operators in optimizing power efficiency, reducing downtime, and scaling operations. By combining OEM/ODM design expertise with rigorous quality assurance, Digipower continues to advance data center innovations.

results.title: About | Digipower Manufacturing Inc. | New Taipei City
results.text:
About | Digipower Manufacturing Inc. | New Taipei City

top of page

# About Digipower

Founded in 1999, DigiPower began by designing and manufacturing power solutions for OEM and ODM customers worldwide. As a leading rack PDU manufacturer in Taiwan, we focus on data center power distribution and management products, including rack PDUs, intelligent PDUs, and IP power meters.

​

Our products are designed to integrate power management for commercial data centers and industrial systems. The main product lines include Basic PDUs, Metered PDUs, IP Monitored PDUs, Switched PDUs, and IP power meters, providing efficient and cost-effective remote power management solutions for our customers.

​

At DigiPower, safety and reliability in the power industry are our top priorities. We are committed to delivering high-quality, stable power products that undergo rigorous testing and certification processes.

​

DigiPower supports partners around the world in winning key infrastructure projects in sectors such as stock exchanges, government agencies, data centers, and global enterprises. Our end customers include H.K. MTR, the Tel Aviv Stock Exchange, IBM France, beIN Sports Qatar, the United Nations, Telefónica, and Singapore Metro, making DigiPower a trustworthy and reliable partner.

results.title: Switched PDU | digipower-1
results.text:
Switched PDU | digipower-1

top of page

# Switched PDU

Switched PDU allows an administrator to access the rack PDU from a remote terminal and interface with it to turn on or off outlets and control load by web and SNMP. This can be helpful if a remote machine has gone into an unresponsive state and cannot be restarted though normal means. An administrator can connect to the PDU the machine is plugged into to power cycle the machine.

##### Features

###### Common

###### Current Measurement

Measure PDU total current consumption

###### Mail Notification

Notify user by mail and SNMP when outlet action or current over threshold

###### Central Management

Free centralized monitoring software, real-time monitoring of a large number of PDU

###### Overload Protection

When overload, breaker will be tripped to protect the safety of electricity

###### Power Sequence

User-definable power on/off sequence time

###### Threshold Sensing

User-definable warning and overload threshold value as the basis for alarm

###### Event Alert

If current exceeds the preset threshold, PDU will beep and LED will flash

###### Available by Model

###### Group Control

Set multiple outlets as a single group while controlling the power output.

###### Environment Detection

Optional accessory probe to detect temperature and humidity.

###### Ping Event Control

No response by PING, the output will perform a preset power action.

###### kWh Measurement

Voltage, frequency, PF, active power, appare

## Company Overview

'DigiPower is a wonderful Taiwanese rack power company. ' quoted from one of our customer. We position ourselves as a leading designer, manufacturer, and distributor of cutting edge digital power management and power conversion for the IT industry. Dedicated to our mission of "EPOWER Natural Excellence", we provide our customers with the most efficient, reliable, industrial-standard and user friendly digital power products to be found anywhere in the market. Established in 1999, DigiPower has enjoyed 8 years of continual growth in the digital power management market and rack power conversion market. Both Power On / Off Switches over IP and rack UPS products have grown to become an integral component for optimizing enterprise power management and rack power conversion. Vertiv™ PowerIT Engineered-to-Order

Rack PDUs

With the widest variety of rack PDUs (rPDUs) and customization options available,

Vertiv PowerIT is the leader in data center power distribution. From basic units to advanced intelligent units, Vertiv offers power solutions for all applications.

Vertiv™ PowerIT Engineered-to-Order Rack PDUs

Factory-configured or a new Geist™ Rack PDU design to meet unique end-user requirements

Vertiv enables users to design their own solution custom-tailored for their application. Our dedicated on-site engineering, reliability, conformance, compliance, and sales support teams provide customers with direct access to participate in designing and manufacturing unique rPDU configurations to meet their requirements. Each unit is built and approved in our labs before being released to production. The Vertiv™ on-site metal fabrication and painting facility streamlines custom designs and color options, reducing the time from design to delivery of engineered-to-order products.

results.title: Data Centre PDU Migration & Rack Security | European Bank Case Study
results.text:
Data Centre PDU Migration & Rack Security | European Bank Case Study

# Breaking Vendor Lock-In for a European Bank

### How a major high-street bank transitioned PDU vendors without losing control or visibility

When a leading European bank decided to change its intelligent PDU supplier, the challenge extended far beyond hardware replacement. Furthermore, the bank operated multiple data centre sites and relied on consistent power monitoring and strict rack-level security. Any disruption or fragmentation in management tools was not an option.

Undoubtedly the priority was clear: move to a new PDU vendor while maintaining a single, unified control platform.

REQUEST CASE STUDY

Valid corporate email address

## The Challenge: Change Without Complexity

The bank needed to replace its existing intelligent PDUs after a vendor partnership ended. However, removing all legacy hardware at once was neither practical nor desirable.

Furthermore, running two separate management systems in parallel would increase operational risk, slow response times, and add unnecessary complexity. At the same time, the bank required fully managed rack access control across all sites, with clear governance, auditing, and accountability.

The solution had to support both legacy and new PDUs simultaneously, while keeping power monitoring, environmental data, and security workflows under one roof.

## A Unified Platform for a Phased Transition

Advanced Datacentre Systems (ADS) delivered a vendor-neutral s

results.title: Legacy Power Monitoring & DCIM for UAE Colocation Provider
results.text:
Legacy Power Monitoring & DCIM for UAE Colocation Provider

# Bridging the Legacy Gap in a UAE Data Centre

### How a leading colocation provider gained unified power visibility without replacing legacy infrastructure

When a major UAE colocation provider acquired a legacy data centre, they inherited more than just racks and customers. They also inherited outdated infrastructure that offered little visibility into power usage. The challenge was clear: support existing clients today while building a smarter, future-ready facility for tomorrow.

REQUEST CASE STUDY

Valid corporate email address

## The Challenge: No Visibility, No Flexibility

The inherited data centre relied entirely on legacy, non-intelligent PDUs. Furthermore, these devices provided no insight into energy consumption at rack level.

This created two immediate risks.

Firstly, the provider could not accurately track or allocate power usage for billing. Secondly, they had no early-warning indicators to detect irregular power behaviour. As a result it increased the likelihood of unexpected outages and operational risk.

At the same time, the provider needed to work within a limited transition budget. A full rip-and-replace of existing hardware was not an option. Unquestionably any solution had to work with what was already in place, while still supporting a gradual move toward intelligent monitoring.

## Why Traditional DCIM Approaches Fell Short

The provider engaged several well-known DCIM and intelligent PDU

results.title: Case Study
results.text:
Raritan Europe, Jan van Galenstraat 59, 3115 JG Schiedam, The Netherlands sales.europe@raritan.com Raritan.eu

##### Customer

Exponential-e, the leading U.K. provider of networking and cloud services.

##### Challenge

To support its new 71,500-square foot (1,625m2) colocation data centre business, Exponential-e needed a responsive and efficient way to monitor the health of the entire data centre and the individual equipment cabinets used by its enterprise customers. Exponential-e also wanted to provide its 1700+ customers advanced monitoring tools for managing their data centre equipment and cabinets remotely.

##### Solution

Power IQ® DCIM Monitoring Software and PX® 5000 Series Intelligent PDUs

##### Results

• Improved data centre manage ment by keeping accurate track of power usage and capacity. 
• Early-warning alerts of potential power issues. 
• Access to real-time energy information to help meet SLAs and plan for future power needs. 
• Provide customers with easy to-use power management and monitoring tools. 
• One of the industry’s lowest Power Usage Effectiveness (PUE) ratings. 

## Case Study

results.title: Telefónica Germany upgrades data center operations with Vertiv™ PowerIT rPDUs for enhanced real-time monitoring
results.text:
#### Case study

##### Background

Telefónica is a leading global telecommunications provider offering mobile, broadband, and digital services worldwide. In Germany, Telefónica is responsible for the operation and modernization of all data centers and core network sites nationwide, maintaining critical infrastructure that serves millions of customers, as well as companies, through its extensive network coverage and innovative solutions. Reliable data center operations are essential to Telefónica’s ability to serve its customers. Critical components, such as power distribution, cooling, monitoring, and control systems, form the backbone of Telefónica’s data center infrastructure in Germany.

### Challenge

Telefónica needed to gather real-time environmental, power, and thermal data across a large number of data centers and core network sites. Continuous monitoring down to rack and outlet level was required to improve transparency, uptime, and operational efficiency.

Company profile: 
Leading telecommunications 
provider. 
Industry: Telecommunications. 
Region: Germany 

## Telefónica Germany upgrades data center operations with Vertiv™ PowerIT rPDUs for enhanced real-time monitoring

##### Solution

Vertiv and the Telefónica Group have maintained a strong, multi-year partnership across Spain and Latin America, 
deploying energy-efficient critical infrastructure solutions. 
For this collaboration, Vertiv delivered Vertiv™ PowerIT switched rPDUs (rack power distribution units),
